title: Continue product-level repair after execution-bridge fix until community foundation is qualified
objective: Use the latest design truths, audit conclusions, and the newly repaired canonical reply chain as the current baseline. Continue auditing, repairing, validating, and cleaning myproject and community-skill until the foundation can be considered a qualified product base. Do not implement group bootstrap or new workflow features in this task.
frozen_truths: myproject is the community engineering main repo.
community-skill is the only skill-body truth source.
openclaw-for-community is only a template/tool repo and not the skill-body authority.
community server is a general community-level server, not a single-group custom server.
runtime only does minimum-duty judgment and protocol mounting.
skill must keep unified message-envelope packaging on the agent side.
group bootstrap/workflow functionality is intentionally deferred in this task.
the inbound message.posted -> needs_agent_judgment -> local OpenClaw bridge -> canonical reply chain is already repaired and becomes the new baseline.
main_goal: Continue from the repaired baseline and keep repairing myproject and community-skill until the remaining foundation-level design mismatches and old logic residues are removed or properly downgraded, and the community foundation can be judged qualified.

required_work.description: Read the continuation context and the previous run evidence first. Verify that the repaired canonical reply chain is real and use it as the current baseline before doing more changes.
Continue producing a concrete mismatch list between the latest design truths and the actual implementation in myproject and community-skill. Focus only on foundation scope.
Repair remaining myproject foundation gaps around data contracts, server consumption of unified message fields, sessions, events, group sessions, and old residues.
Repair remaining community-skill foundation gaps around protocol mounting, agent-side execution handoff, unified message-envelope packaging, ingress assumptions, and release pollution.
Remove or downgrade misleading legacy residue, stale compatibility assumptions, and defaults that contradict the current design truths.
After every meaningful repair batch, rerun the relevant validation. Do not accept HTTP 200/202 as final proof; use canonical effect and persisted facts.
Continue until the foundation is qualified or the next blocker is real, external, and cannot be solved in the current server-side scope.

in_scope: server consumption of unified message fields
agent protocol and group protocol mounting into the execution chain
release/default configuration pollution cleanup where it affects product foundation
out_of_scope: using openclaw-for-community as the main authority
hard_rules: Do not implement group bootstrap in this task.
Do not expand into new workflow feature development.
Do not treat openclaw-for-community as the skill-body truth source.
Do not cite the outdated claim that community-skill removed session/sync dependency.
Do not collapse runtime into a full manager orchestrator.
Do not delete duplicated author_kind yet; keep it as an audited compatibility residue.
Do not accept HTTP 200/202 as final success.
Do not regress the repaired needs_agent_judgment -> canonical reply chain by replacing it with a fake generic reply.
key_focus_order: 1. Remaining implementation/design mismatches in myproject and community-skill.
2. Remaining weak points around the repaired agent execution bridge.
3. Skill-side message-envelope packaging correctness and residue cleanup.
5. Old logic residue and release/default pollution cleanup.
qualification_bar: Fresh install plus live community validation remains green on onboarding, send, and inbound canonical effect.
No critical foundation-level design mismatch remains open in myproject or community-skill main paths.
No known old logic residue remains on the repaired main path that can invalidate later group-feature work.
The foundation can be handed off for later group/bootstrap implementation without hiding a fresh product blocker.

deliverables.description: Evidence bundle for the continuation run.
